meta.title: Wan 2.2 I2V RunPod Serverless Endpoint — Custom Fork
meta.scope: Build a reliable, well-tested RunPod serverless endpoint for Wan 2.2 image-to-video generation with LoRA support. Forked from wlsdml1114/generate_video (which has broken ksampler, missing parameter wiring, poor error handling). Our fork fixes all known issues, adds local testing capability, and produces a Docker image deployable to RunPod serverless.
non_goals: Training or fine-tuning models — we use pre-trained Wan 2.2 + community LoRAs
Real-time generation — serverless is async (submit job, poll for result)
GUI or web interface — API-only, consumed by our zone2 pipeline
Supporting GPUs below 24GB VRAM — targeting A100/4090/5090
assumptions: RunPod account with API key and serverless endpoint access
Network volume with LoRA .safetensors files at /runpod-volume/loras/
Docker Hub or GitHub Container Registry for pushing custom images
ComfyUI as the inference backend (runs inside the Docker container)
Mac Studio M2 Ultra 64GB available for local workflow validation (slow but functional with GGUF models)

hardware.local_testing: Mac Studio M2 Ultra 64GB — ComfyUI with GGUF quantized models (Q5_K_M), ~15-30 min per video but validates workflow JSON correctness

success_criteria.primary: Submit a job via RunPod API with base64 image + prompt + LoRA pairs, get back a base64 video with visible motion/animation
success_criteria.stretch: Full end-to-end test: local ComfyUI validates workflow JSON, same workflow deploys to RunPod and produces identical results
success_criteria.reliability: Zero HTTP 400 errors from ComfyUI — all parameter validation happens in handler before queue_prompt

phases.title: Local ComfyUI Setup & Workflow Validation
phases.description: Get ComfyUI running locally on Mac Studio with GGUF-quantized Wan 2.2 models. Validate that the workflow JSON we will use in production actually works — catch node errors, missing custom nodes, and parameter issues locally before deploying to RunPod.

tasks.title: Install ComfyUI on Mac Studio
tasks.description: Clone ComfyUI, install Python dependencies, verify it starts and serves on port 8188. Install with MPS (Metal) backend support.
acceptance: MPS backend is detected (or CPU fallback works)

tasks.title: Install required ComfyUI custom nodes
tasks.description: Install the same custom nodes used by the production workflow: ComfyUI-GGUF, ComfyUI-KJNodes, ComfyUI-VideoHelperSuite, ComfyUI-WanVideoWrapper. These are needed for the v1.0.6-style workflow that uses WanVideoSampler, WanVideoModelLoader, etc.
acceptance: ComfyUI restarts cleanly with all nodes available

tasks.title: Download GGUF-quantized Wan 2.2 I2V models
tasks.description: Download Q5_K_M GGUF variants of Wan 2.2 I2V high-noise and low-noise models from HuggingFace. Also download the text encoder, VAE, and CLIP vision model. Place in correct ComfyUI model directories.
acceptance: umt5-xxl text encoder available (bf16 or fp8)

tasks.title: Create GGUF-adapted workflow JSON for local testing
tasks.description: Adapt the production workflow (new_Wan22_api.json from v1.0.6) to use GGUF model loaders instead of the standard WanVideoModelLoader. The GGUF node replaces the safetensor loader but the rest of the workflow (sampling, encoding, LoRA, output) stays the same. This lets us validate the workflow structure locally.
acceptance: Uses UnetLoaderGGUF instead of WanVideoModelLoader for the diffusion models
All other nodes match the production workflow
Workflow loads in ComfyUI without errors (check /prompt endpoint or GUI)

tasks.title: Generate a test video locally
tasks.description: Submit the local workflow to ComfyUI via its API (POST /prompt) with a test image. Verify that a video is produced. This will be slow (30+ min) but proves the workflow JSON is valid and all nodes connect properly.
acceptance: POST /prompt accepts the workflow without HTTP 400
ComfyUI processes the workflow (may take 15-60 min on Mac)

phases.title: Production Handler & Workflow
phases.description: Build the production handler.py and workflow JSON that will run inside the RunPod Docker container. Fix all the issues from the upstream repo: proper parameter wiring, input validation, clear error messages, clean LoRA handling.

tasks.title: Create production workflow JSON
tasks.description: Based on the v1.0.6 new_Wan22_api.json but cleaned up. This uses the standard WanVideoModelLoader (fp8 safetensors, not GGUF) since production runs on NVIDIA GPUs. Key nodes: WanVideoModelLoader (HIGH + LOW), WanVideoSampler (two-pass), WanVideoLoraSelectMulti (HIGH + LOW), WanVideoContextOptions, WanVideoTextEncode, WanVideoImageToVideoEncode, WanVideoDecode, VHS_VideoCombine.
acceptance: Uses fp8 safetensor model files (same as v1.0.6)
lora_1 through lora_4 slots available for user LoRAs
All node IDs documented in handler comments

tasks.title: Write handler.py — the RunPod serverless handler
tasks.description: Clean rewrite of the handler. Fixes from upstream: (1) All params use .get() with sane defaults — never crash on missing input. (2) seed, cfg, steps are properly wired to workflow nodes. (3) Input validation before queue_prompt — catch bad params early with clear errors. (4) Base64 image handling is robust. (5) LoRA pairs properly applied to WanVideoLoraSelectMulti nodes. (6) Better logging with request IDs.
acceptance: handler.py accepts: image (base64/url/path), prompt, negative_prompt, width (default 480), height (default 832), length (default 81), steps (default 8), seed (default random), cfg (default 2.0), context_overlap (default 48), lora_pairs (default [])
All params validated before submitting to ComfyUI
seed/cfg/steps properly written to workflow node inputs
LoRA pairs applied to nodes 279 (HIGH) and 553 (LOW), lora_1 through lora_4
Returns {error: descriptive_message} on failure — never a raw traceback

tasks.title: Write entrypoint.sh
tasks.description: Shell script that starts ComfyUI in the background, waits for it to be ready, then starts the handler. Same pattern as upstream but with better error handling and timeout logging.
acceptance: Waits up to 120s for ComfyUI HTTP to respond
Exits with error if ComfyUI fails to start

tasks.title: Write extra_model_paths.yaml
tasks.description: ComfyUI config that maps model directories, including the RunPod network volume at /runpod-volume/ for user LoRAs and any custom models.

tasks.title: Write generate_video_client.py — local API client
tasks.description: Clean Python client for submitting jobs to the RunPod endpoint. Handles base64 encoding, job submission, polling, and video download. This replaces the runpod_client.py in zone2/image_gen/ with a standalone version that can be used independently.
acceptance: GenerateVideoClient class with submit_job, wait_for_completion, save_video methods
Proper base64 encoding of input images
CLI mode: python generate_video_client.py --image test.png --prompt 'motion description'

phases.title: Docker Image & RunPod Deployment
phases.description: Build the Docker image with all dependencies baked in, push to registry, and deploy as a RunPod serverless endpoint.

tasks.title: Write Dockerfile
tasks.description: Based on the v1.0.6 Dockerfile but cleaned up. Uses a suitable CUDA base image, installs ComfyUI + custom nodes, downloads model weights, copies handler/workflow/entrypoint. Models baked into the image to avoid cold-start downloads.
acceptance: Wan 2.2 fp8 model weights downloaded during build
CLIP vision, T5 text encoder, VAE downloaded during build
handler.py, workflow/, entrypoint.sh, extra_model_paths.yaml copied in

tasks.title: Test Docker image locally (optional — needs NVIDIA GPU)
tasks.description: If an NVIDIA GPU is available, test the Docker image locally with nvidia-docker. If not, skip this and test directly on RunPod. On Mac, we can at least verify the image builds and the entrypoint script logic.
acceptance: If NVIDIA GPU available: container starts, ComfyUI responds, handler accepts a test job
If no NVIDIA GPU: build succeeds, entrypoint.sh is executable, handler.py imports cleanly

tasks.title: Push Docker image to registry
tasks.description: Push the built image to Docker Hub or GitHub Container Registry so RunPod can pull it. Tag with version number.
acceptance: Image pushed to registry with tag v1.0.0
Registry URL documented for RunPod template creation

tasks.title: Create RunPod serverless endpoint with custom image
tasks.description: Create a new RunPod serverless endpoint using our custom Docker image. Configure with appropriate GPU type, network volume for LoRAs, and scaling settings.
acceptance: Uses our custom Docker image from registry

phases.title: End-to-End Testing & Integration
phases.description: Test the full pipeline end-to-end: submit real video generation jobs, verify output quality, test LoRA loading, and integrate with the zone2 pipeline.

tasks.title: Verify endpoint health and worker readiness
tasks.description: Hit the /health endpoint, verify workers spin up, confirm ComfyUI is responding inside the container.
acceptance: Worker logs show ComfyUI started successfully
No custom node import errors in logs

tasks.title: Generate video without LoRAs
tasks.description: Submit a basic job: test image + simple motion prompt, no LoRAs. Verify the returned video has actual motion/animation (unlike our previous static results).
acceptance: Video has visible motion (not static frames)
Video is correct resolution and frame count

tasks.title: Generate video with LoRAs
tasks.description: Submit a job with our titfuck LoRA pair (HIGH + LOW). Use appropriate trigger words in the prompt. Verify the LoRA effect is visible in the output.
acceptance: Job completes with 1 LoRA pair specified
No 'LoRA not found' or 'Value not in list' errors

tasks.title: Test parameter edge cases
tasks.description: Test various parameter combinations to verify handler robustness: missing params (should use defaults), bad values (should return error not crash), different resolutions, different step counts, random seeds.
acceptance: Missing seed/cfg/steps: uses defaults, job succeeds
Invalid width (e.g. 0, negative): returns descriptive error
No crashes or raw tracebacks in any test

tasks.title: Update zone2 runpod_client.py to use new endpoint
tasks.description: Update the zone2/image_gen/runpod_client.py to point at our new custom endpoint. Verify the existing generate_scene_library.py pipeline works end-to-end with the new endpoint.
acceptance: runpod_client.py updated with new endpoint ID
generate_scene_library.py can submit a job and get a video back
frame_extractor.py can extract frames from the result
